manual_next_steps_supported_by_local_evi: Review relation endpoint-shape records for either a corrected allowed relation kind or a parent project/corridor subject, using original source evidence before changing submissions.

Prioritize identity clusters with negative review reasons as likely do-not-merge review candidates, but do not auto-apply because validator duplicate keys can mix true aliases with route variants, borough-specific community boards, and corridors with different limits.

Re-run or replace the current identity-review suggestions only through an authorized model-backed or human review path, then apply reviewed decisions through the project identity override/canonicalization flow.
no_canonical_edits_made_reason: This shard was explicitly limited to Codex-only inspection and review notes. I did not run forbidden canonicalize, dedup, identity-review, write, ask, schema, ontology, or provider-profile commands, and I did not edit canonical, submission, wiki, LOG, or generated files. Applying fixes would require canonical/submission mutations or model-backed identity decisions outside this write scope.

author: codex
scope: manual canonical cleanup: 125th Street corridor duplicate identity cluster
applied_decisions: Alias corridor_125th-st-crosstown-phase2-candidate to corridor_125th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2 because both source records have the same corridor name, borough, BRT Phase II candidate scope, and matching evidence block hash.

Add do-not-merge guardrail for corridor_125th-st-manhattan and corridor_125th-street-manhattan because their corridor limits differ: Morningside Avenue to Second Avenue versus Amsterdam Avenue to Second Avenue.

ambiguous_pairs.reason: Phase II candidate record has no explicit limits, while the SBS/M60 corridor record has concrete limits and project context; broad study corridor versus specific SBS segment is not settled by current evidence.
Same 125th Street Manhattan theme, but Phase II candidate lacks limits and is tied to BRT Phase II study context while the other record is tied to M60 SBS / 125th Street Transportation Improvements.
